text: <par>Although the second syllable of this Mongsen Ao form, <latinform>tə-sə-maŋ</latinform>, appears to reflect the PTB <latinform>*s-</latinform> prefix, in reality the form is a compound of the roots <latinform>sə</latinform> 'die' and <latinform>maŋ</latinform> 'body', with the nominal prefix <latinform>tə-</latinform>.</par>
<par>Ao drums are made from large, hollowed-out tree trunks (cf. Mills 1926).</par>
<par>cf. PKC <latinform>*yuŋ</latinform> FINGER / TOE / ROOT (mesoroot of <xref ref="320">#320</xref>).</par>
<par>Original source contains <latinform>thijàʔ</latinform>, which Coupe has since corrected to <latinform>tihjàʔ</latinform> (p.c. to dwbruhn, 2012-07-14).</par>
<par>Coupe revises this gloss to 'taro' (p.c. Daniel Bruhn 2013).</par>
